purpose: Add isAllowedDmSender whitelist checks before every side-effect in the two inbound DM subscribers (directMessageNotifications.ts and ContactChat.tsx). Gates protect rememberContact, incrementDirectMessage, dedup-set population, appendMessage/upsertMessages, and kind-7 applyInboundRumor. Adds INFO-level drop logging and removes the bug-documenting comment.

public_api.name: subscribeDirectMessageNotifications
public_api.signature: function subscribeDirectMessageNotifications(params: { ndk: NDK; ownPubkeyHex: string; privateKeyHex: string; isAllowedSender: (peer: string) => boolean }): () => void
public_api.description: Modified signature: adds isAllowedSender callback parameter. Caller (DirectMessageNotificationsWatcher) passes (peer) => isAllowedDmSender(peer, groups, ownPubkeyHex) built from useMarmot().groups.

design_decisions.isAllowedSender_injection: subscribeDirectMessageNotifications receives isAllowedSender as a callback parameter rather than computing it internally. This keeps the function free of React context dependencies (lib/ boundary rule) and allows the caller to inject the current group snapshot via closure.
design_decisions.DirectMessageNotificationsWatcher_change: DirectMessageNotificationsWatcher.tsx is updated to call useMarmot() to get groups, then pass (peer) => isAllowedDmSender(peer, groups, ownPubkeyHex) to subscribeDirectMessageNotifications. This is the minimal change required to thread groups into the notification path.
design_decisions.ContactChat_gate: ContactChat.tsx already has access to MarmotContext via the component tree. It calls useMarmot() to get groups and constructs isAllowedDmSender checks inline at each of the three handler gate points.
design_decisions.dedup_gate_ordering: The whitelist check in directMessageNotifications.ts runs BEFORE seenMessageIds/seenRumorIds population. A stranger event must not occupy a dedup slot — if it did, a later member re-delivery of the same event id would be falsely deduplicated (AC-SEC-4, AC-SEC-5).
design_decisions.logging: Stranger drop events are logged via the existing createLogger('dm') logger at INFO level with tag 'dm:walled-garden-drop'. The log includes the first 8 characters of the sender pubkey and the event kind. No message content or inner rumor body is included (AC-OBS-1).
